title: Sieve Card v1 — Shaping Layer
intent: Name the shaping layer that turns raw source material or source artifacts into the next candidate artifact with visible preservation, declared loss, and explicit check surfaces.

context.problem: Boot, schema, and check can govern a substrate, but the protocol also needs a named shaping layer that decides what survives from raw material into the next artifact.
context.scope: Core card set admission. Detailed proving profiles and host-specific sieve variants can evolve separately.

transform.input_shape: Raw source material, annotated excerpts, or source artifacts containing more information than the next artifact should carry.
transform.sieve_rule: Shape source material into a candidate artifact by declaring what is preserved, what is dropped, what loss is declared, and what checks must witness the result before trust.
transform.output_shape: Candidate artifact plus visible preservation and loss posture, with an explicit check surface.

outcome.output_success: A reviewer can inspect the resulting artifact and tell what the sieve preserved, what it dropped, and what still requires check.
outcome.outcome_success: Sieve is available as canonical protocol vocabulary instead of remaining implicit inside transforms or host-specific tooling.

constraints: A sieve must declare what it preserves, not only what it drops.
A sieve may compress, but it may not silently upgrade compression into truth.
A sieve must emit or point to an explicit check surface.
A sieve must preserve lineage back to source material or source artifacts.
A canonical sieve card should stay simpler than the proving profiles that justify it.

governance.note: Sieve is part of the current core card set and remains subject to the same promotion and supersession rules as other cards.

drop: host-specific noise not needed for the next artifact
declare_loss: ambiguities deferred to later artifacts or checks

check_surface.failure_gate: If the shaping result hides loss, loses lineage, or bypasses check, it should not be trusted or promoted.

evaluation.output_success: The current core card set includes a named shaping layer.
evaluation.outcome_success: Tools and hosts can invoke canonical Sieve directly when running shaping or promotion tasks.

degrade_policy.rule: A cold reader must still be able to tell what Sieve is for and why it remains coupled to Check.
